-

вАЬfather

and

.

4
In 2021, the Bank of
England released a
new ¬£50 banknote
featuring Alan Turing

вАҐ

A

Turing

machine

(TM)

is

an

abstract

model

of

a

computer

first
proposed

by

Alan

Turing

in

1936
.
вАҐ

A

Turing

machine

can

do

everything

that

a

real

computer

can

do
.
вАҐ

A

Turing

machine

is

similar

to

a

finite

automaton

but

with

an
unlimited

and

unrestricted

memory

in

the

form

of

a

tape
.
вАҐ

The

tape

is

infinite
.
вАҐ

The

machine

can

both

write

on

the

tape

and

read

from

it
.
вАҐ

The

read
-
write

head

can

move

to

the

left

and

to

the

right
.

5

вАҐ

How

does

a

Turing

machine

work?
вАҐ

It

uses

an

infinite

tape

as

its

unlimited

memory
.
вАҐ

It

has

a

head

that

can

read

and

write

symbols

and

move

around

the

tape
.
вАҐ

Initially,

the

head

is

at

the

start

of

the

tape

and

the

tape

contains

only

the
input

string

and

is

blank

everywhere

else
.
вАҐ

If

the

machine

needs

to

store

information,

it

may

write

it

on

the

tape
.
вАҐ

The

machine

immediately

accepts

if

it

enters

the

accept

state

and

it
immediately

rejects

if

it

enters

the

reject

state
.
вАҐ

If

the

machine

doesnвАЩt

enter

the

accept

or

reject

state
,

it

will

loop

forever
.

вАҐ

A

Turing

machine

accepts

an

input

if

it

enters

the

accept

state
.
вАҐ

The

final

contents

of

the

tape

and

the

final

position

of

the

head

do

not

matter
.
вАҐ

It

doesnвАЩt

even

have

to

finish

reading

the

input
.
вАҐ

Similarly,

a

machine

rejects

an

input

if

it

enters

the

reject

state
.
вАҐ

Possible

outcomes

of

a

computation

with

a

Turing

machine
:
вАҐ

The

computation

halts
.
вАҐ

It

enters

the

accept

state

and

accepts
.
вАҐ

It

enters

the

reject

state

and

rejects
.
вАҐ

The

computation

does

not

halt

(i
.
e
.
,

it

loops

forever
)
.
вАҐ

It

never

enters

the

accept

or

reject

state
.

structuredContent.title: Turing Machines (Part I)
structuredContent.summary: Introduction to Turing machines as the most powerful class of automata, capable of recognizing languages beyond regular and context-free languages. Covers formal definition, components, transitions, and acceptance conditions.

sections.heading: Language Hierarchy
items: Regular languages: recognized by DFAs/NFAs
Context-free languages: recognized by PDAs
Beyond CFLs: require Turing machines
Example: L = {w#w | w вИИ {0,1}*} is neither regular nor context-free

sections.heading: Turing Machine Components
items: Infinite tape (unlimited memory)
Read-write head that moves left and right
State control unit
Can read, write, and move in both directions
Initially: head at start, tape contains input + blanks

sections.heading: Formal Definition
items: TM is 7-tuple: (Q, ќ£, ќУ, ќі, qвВА, qвВРccвВСвВЪвВЬ, qбµ£вВСв±ЉвВСcвВЬ)
Q: finite set of states
ќ£: input alphabet (no blank symbol вКФ)
ќУ: tape alphabet (вКФ вИИ ќУ, ќ£ вКЖ ќУ)
ќі: Q' √Ч ќУ вЖТ Q √Ч ќУ √Ч {L,R} (transition function)
Q' = Q - {qвВРccвВСвВЪвВЬ, qбµ£вВСв±ЉвВСcвВЬ}
qвВРccвВСвВЪвВЬ вЙ† qбµ£вВСв±ЉвВСcвВЬ

sections.heading: Transitions
items: Format: q вЖТ r on aвЖТb,R
Read symbol a, write symbol b, move right (R) or left (L)
ќі(q,a) = (r,b,R) means: state q reading a вЖТ state r, write b, move right
Each move changes: current state, tape symbol, head position

sections.heading: Acceptance Conditions
items: Accept: enters qвВРccвВСвВЪвВЬ state
Reject: enters qбµ£вВСв±ЉвВСcвВЬ state
Loop forever: never enters accept/reject states
Final tape contents and head position don't matter for acceptance

sections.heading: Configurations
items: Format: uqv where u is left of head, q is state, v is head+right
Start configuration: qвВАw (w is input)
Accepting configuration: contains qвВРccвВСвВЪвВЬ state
Configuration sequence shows computation path

questionsToReview: How do you represent a transition where TM reads 0, writes 1, moves left?
What are the three possible outcomes of a TM computation?
Given a TM diagram, trace through execution on a specific input
What is the start configuration for input 'aaba'?
How does TM power compare to DFA and PDA capabilities?
Why can't DFAs or PDAs recognize L = {w#w | w вИИ {0,1}*}?
